title: Non-typical pay structures
description: Practice identifying non-typical pay positions and understanding the tax implications of self-employment.

assessment_items.question_content:
Luke is a software engineer at a tech company, who gets a monthly salary. 

**What type of worker is Luke?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Sara is a graphic designer who works on different design projects for several clients she chooses.

** What type of worker is Sara?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Ana is a plumbing specialist who does all the plumbing work for a local construction company's projects based on a long-term agreement. 

** What type of worker is Ana?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Gig employment has recently become a popular way for people to earn income.

**Which of these is a pro of gig employment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You can set your own work hours.
Your income is the same every month.
You get benefits like health insurance.
You need to find your own customers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gig employment has recently become a popular way for people to earn income.

**What's pro of gig work?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your work can be anything you are good at and enjoy.
You have a boss who tells you what to do.
If you get sick, you still get paid.
You always work the same hours every day.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gig employment has recently become a popular way for people to earn income.

**What could be a tough part, or con, of gig work?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: If you don't work, you don't earn money.
You choose who to work for.
You can do more than one gig job at a time.

assessment_items.question_content:
When you are self-employed, your taxes are calculated a bit differently than if you were an employee.

**In addition to income tax, what other taxes are self-employed workers required to pay?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

Nick has recently started his independent graphic design business.

**Besides income tax, which additional tax does Nick need to pay as a self-employed person?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

Jane is an independent contractor who works as a consultant for various companies.

**What tax does Jane need to pay in addition to income tax?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

You are a freelance writer and work from your home office. 

**Which of the following could you claim as a business expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a gaming console for your brother
a microwave to cook your lunch

assessment_items.question_content:
You work for yourself as a freelance photographer. 

**Which of the following could you potentially claim as a business expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a vacation trip with your family
new clothes for your personal wardrobe
a birthday gift for your spouse

assessment_items.question_content:
You are a self-employed fitness trainer and work out of your home. 

**As a self-employed fitness trainer, which of these would be considered a business expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a new phone for your personal use
fitness equipment for your home studio
a weekend trip to the beach
